title: InstCombine: Infer nnan and ninf on fpext
summary:
The developers generalized the optimization by extracting the constant-folding logic from `SimplifyDemandedUseFPClass` into a reusable helper function. Initially, the pattern only handled `fpext` directly, folding it to a constant when the demanded mask and known classes aligned. The patch moves this logic into `simplifyDemandedFPClassResult`, which now accepts any `Instruction*` instead of just `CallInst*`. This allows the same transformation to be applied uniformly across different opcodes, like `fpext`, while maintaining fast-math flag handling and constant creation.

The generalization enables the optimization to cover diverse `nofpclass` constraints and operand patterns (selects, calls, direct values) by decoupling the folding step from the specific instruction analysis. Developers can apply this by identifying common result-simplification patterns, extracting them into a shared function, and invoking it from multiple instruction-specific handlers, ensuring consistent behavior and reducing code duplication.
